expectations.text: The fetch step has a per-step ```config block with a `retry:` policy: `max: 5` and `backoff: exponential`
expectations.evidence: Lines 46-53: `retry: {max: 5, delay: 1s, backoff: exponential, jitter: true}` — note: `jitter: true` is likely wrong type (spec expects 0..1 numeric) but max/backoff are correct

expectations.text: Includes a python script step (```python code block) for the JSON transform
expectations.evidence: ## transform uses a ```bash block that invokes `python3 -` via heredoc inline. There is no ```python step block, so the transform is actually a bash step, not a python step. The user asked for 'run a python script to transform'.

expectations.text: Has a failure branch that routes to a `mail`-based notify step — either via a `fail` edge or a `fail:max` exhaustion handler
expectations.evidence: Lines 30, 33, 35: `fetch_sales -->|fail| notify_ops`, `transform -->|fail| notify_ops`, `upload -->|fail| notify_ops`; notify_ops uses `mail -s`

expectations.text: If `label max:N` appears on any edge, it's paired with a matching `label:max` handler edge (or retries are expressed via step-level config only)
expectations.evidence: No edge-level max:N; step-level retry only

expectations.text: Every node in the mermaid flow has a matching `## <id>` heading and vice versa
expectations.evidence: fetch_sales, transform, upload, done, notify_ops all present in both

expectations.text: `flags:` lists (if any) don't duplicate the agent's non-interactive switch (`-p`, `exec -`)
No `local` or `global` keys nested inside a `RESULT` JSON
expectations.evidence: No RESULT sentinels emitted; steps rely on exit-code routing
